bullet_points: Write out the general DHOST Lagrangian and identify all relevant terms.
Expand the action to quadratic order in perturbations around a flat background in unitary gauge.
Isolate the kinetic matrix for the propagating degrees of freedom.
Carefully document and verify all assumptions made when isolating the kinetic matrix, cross-checking with symbolic computation tools where possible.
Compute the determinant of the kinetic matrix and express the degeneracy condition explicitly in terms of the Lagrangian functions.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Derive the general degeneracy condition for the DHOST Lagrangian (Eq. 2.8, arXiv:1811.06271)

bullet_points: Clearly separate the substitution of Type Ia relations (from arXiv:1602.08398) into the general degeneracy condition from the subsequent stability analysis.
Show explicitly that the degeneracy condition is satisfied for Type Ia.
Analyze the quadratic action for scalar and tensor perturbations on an FLRW background.
Explicitly label and document the code/calculation steps where the dispersion relations are computed and plotted.
Derive the dispersion relations and propagation speeds for physical modes.
Confirm the absence of ghost and gradient instabilities at tree level, making it easy to identify the impact of the degeneracy condition.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Restrict to Type Ia subclass and verify degeneracy, then analyze classical stability

bullet_points: Propose a general non-linear, field-dependent transformation for the scalar and metric fields.
Compute the variation of the Type Ia DHOST action under this transformation.
Include intermediate checks: for example, verify that small perturbations in the transformation functions reproduce the degeneracy condition.
Derive the conditions for invariance and show their equivalence to the degeneracy condition, validating each step.
Solve for the explicit transformation functions generating the symmetry.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Investigate the existence of a hidden field-dependent gauge symmetry in Type Ia DHOST

bullet_points: Use the path integral formalism to derive the Ward-Takahashi identities associated with the symmetry.
Systematically document the derivation and list all possible one-loop counterterms.
Compare the structure of the one-loop counterterms with known stability conditions in the literature.
Apply the Ward identities to constrain the structure of radiative corrections.
Demonstrate explicitly how the symmetry forbids the generation of ghostly or destabilizing terms at one-loop.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Derive Ward identities and analyze one-loop quantum stability

bullet_points: Impose the additional constraints to reduce the Lagrangian to Beyond Horndeski and Horndeski forms.
Re-evaluate the degeneracy condition, transformation functions, and Ward identities in these limits.

Add explicit commentary within the computation on how the transition to these limits modifies or simplifies the symmetry structure, clearly marking any differences in the degeneracy condition and transformation functions.
Determine whether the symmetry persists and continues to protect against instabilities.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Analyze the symmetry and stability mechanism in the Horndeski and Beyond Horndeski limits

bullet_points: Summarize the explicit derivation of the degeneracy condition and its satisfaction in Type Ia.
Discuss the demonstration of classical stability (absence of ghosts/gradients), referencing the labeled plots and calculations.
Present the discovery and explicit form of the field-dependent gauge symmetry and its equivalence to degeneracy, including validation steps.
Interpret the Ward identities and their implications for quantum stability at one-loop, referencing the systematic counterterm analysis.
Analyze the persistence and role of the symmetry in the Horndeski and Beyond Horndeski limits, including commentary on any modifications.
Include a critical discussion of the limitations and potential extensions of the observed gauge symmetry, referencing both computational results and theoretical derivations.
Reference and interpret all key plots, statistics, and computational results from previous steps to provide a cohesive and comprehensive results section.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Write the full Results section, including all qualitative and quantitative findings, interpretations, and references to plots/statistics
